zh.title: 以太坊/美元技术分析：横盘整理偏多震荡结构
zh.content:
当前ETH/USD处于低波动横盘整理结构，价格围绕1585窄幅震荡，整体略偏多头，短线动能有限但未转弱，需等待方向选择。

技术指标方面，价格站上SMA20（1577）形成短期支撑，MACD维持正值动能，趋势仍偏向震荡上行结构，但信号未完全确认。

下方支撑关注1577及1580附近密集成交区，上方阻力位1588-1595区间，突破需放量确认，否则易反复震荡结构待突破。

操作建议以区间交易为主，回踩1577附近可分批布局多单，若跌破1570需谨慎止损，反弹至阻力区逐步减仓控制风险波动。

en.title: Ethereum vs US Dollar Technical Analysis
en.content:
Ethereum is currently in a tight consolidation phase against the US dollar, trading almost flat around 1585 after a prolonged period of low volatility. Price action shows the market coiling just above recent equilibrium levels, suggesting neither buyers nor sellers have clear dominance. The broader short-term structure leans mildly bullish as price holds above recent averages, but momentum is muted and lacks decisive breakout pressure.

Momentum indicators reflect a market that is stabilizing rather than trending. The MACD remains positive at 2.71712, indicating underlying bullish bias, but the absence of a visible signal line reading limits confirmation of strength. With RSI unavailable, momentum clarity is reduced, so price action becomes more important. The fact that price holds above the 20-period SMA at 1577.453 suggests short-term support is intact.

Price structure reveals a very compressed trading range, with immediate support forming near the intraday low around 1584.04 and stronger structural backing at the 20-period SMA near 1577.453. On the upside, resistance is tightly defined at the recent high of 1585.67, making this a narrow equilibrium zone. A breakout above this level could open room toward the 1590 region, while rejection may retest support.

Given the compressed volatility, trading conditions favor range-bound strategies rather than directional bets. Buying near support around 1577–1584 offers favorable risk-reward with tight stops just below 1575. On the upside, partial profit-taking near 1585.67 is prudent unless a clean breakout occurs with strong momentum. A sustained move above resistance would justify momentum longs targeting 1590 and beyond, but false breakouts remain a key risk.
